meta.device: Sullair LS110 Rotary Screw Air Compressor

sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: P4 always >= P3. Delta increases as filter loads.
sensor_pairs.source: Section 3.4 — fluid filter change when delta exceeds 20psi

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Normal filter loading — approaching service interval
Filter bypass valve has opened — unfiltered oil now reaching air end
correlation_signatures.reasoning: Manual documents integral bypass valve. If delta collapses without filter change, bypass is the only path.
correlation_signatures.risk: HIGH — abrasive particles reaching bearings and rotors

sensor_pairs.description: Oil flow sufficiency vs discharge temperature
sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: As P3 drops (reduced oil flow), T1 rises. Oil is both coolant and sealant in air end.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: Manual section 2.3 states fluid has three functions: coolant, seal, lubricant. Reduced flow (P3 drop) must therefore reduce cooling capacity (T1 rise).

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Fluid filter clogging — reduced oil flow causing thermal rise
correlation_signatures.action: Check P4-P3 delta — if >15psi plan filter change

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Filter bypass valve opened — oil flow restored via bypass but unfiltered
correlation_signatures.risk: HIGH — thermal appears normal but contamination risk is real
correlation_signatures.note: This is the dangerous signature — machine appears healthy on T1 alone

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Thermal issue not related to oil flow — check cooler, ambient, thermal valve

sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: T1 is wet discharge (pre-separator, oil-laden air). T2 is dry discharge (post-separator). T2 should always be lower than T1 — separator strips oil which carries heat.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: Manual section 2.4 and 2.5 document T1 and T2 positions. Oil carries thermal energy. Effective separation removes heat-carrying fluid.

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Separator element degrading — oil carryover increasing
correlation_signatures.controller_message: SEPARATOR MAINT REQD when separator dP > 10psi
correlation_signatures.note: T1-T2 gap closure may precede dP alert — earlier warning signal

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Separator element ruptured — hot oil bypassing directly to discharge
Global thermal issue — not separator specific. Check cooler and thermal valve.

sensor_pairs.description: Thermal envelope model — expected T1 given operating conditions
sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: T1 is a predictable function of load percentage and ambient temperature. Deviation from expected value indicates developing fault before any threshold is breached.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: Thermodynamic relationship between compression ratio, inlet conditions and discharge temperature is well-established physics. Specific correction factors require validation against real machine data.

efficiency_correction_range.note: Requires calibration from real machine data

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Thermal system degrading — 6°F above model prediction suggests developing restriction in oil circuit or cooler fouling
Thermal valve stuck open — overcooling at current conditions
correlation_signatures.risk: condensation and fluid foaming at sustained low temperatures

sensor_pairs.description: Sump-to-line pressure relationship during load transitions
sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: During load increase, P2 rises and P1 tracks within a known band. Divergence indicates flow restriction between sump and discharge.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: Manual section 2.5 documents minimum pressure valve maintains 50psi floor. P1 and P2 are hydraulically coupled through the separator and minimum pressure valve.

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Separator restriction growing — pressure building in sump faster than it can pass through to line
Minimum pressure valve issue — sump not tracking line pressure correctly
Solenoid valve or blowdown valve failure — unload logic not functioning
correlation_signatures.source: Section 7.16.2 troubleshooting — line pressure rises above unload setpoint

sensor_pairs.description: Inlet vs fluid system restriction differentiation
sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: PSW1 indicates inlet air restriction. P3 indicates fluid circuit restriction. They are hydraulically independent — one can rise without the other.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: PSW1 is on the inlet air path. P3 is on the fluid injection path. Different circuits — simultaneous rise indicates environmental cause.

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Inlet filter clogging — air restriction only. Clean or replace inlet filter.
correlation_signatures.source: Section 7.16.2 — dirty air filter under compressor will not build full discharge pressure

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Fluid circuit restriction — fluid filter or thermal valve. Air path clear.
Environmental contamination — high particulate environment affecting both air and fluid systems simultaneously. Check installation environment.
correlation_signatures.risk: Installation problem rather than component fault — filter changes alone will not solve

sensor_pairs.description: Detects upstream demand changes that reweight all failure chains
sensor_pairs.expected_behaviour: Stable operation has consistent load% for given P2 setpoint. Sudden sustained increase in load% at same P2 indicates new downstream demand — all degradation timelines accelerate.
sensor_pairs.reasoning: Higher sustained load increases thermal stress, accelerates filter loading, increases seal wear rates non-linearly. Load multipliers applied to all active failure chain projections.

load_multipliers.note: SYNTHETIC — requires validation from real degradation data

correlation_signatures.interpretation: Operating regime has shifted — new equipment on line or process change upstream. Recalculate all failure chain projections with new load baseline.
correlation_signatures.agent_action: Flag regime change, reweight all active FC projections, surface accelerated maintenance intervals

composite_diagnostics.name: Silent filter bypass detection
composite_diagnostics.logic: IF (P4_minus_P3 < 3psi) AND (T1 within_normal_range) AND (runtime > 1500hrs since last filter change) THEN filter_bypass_valve_open
composite_diagnostics.danger: Machine appears healthy on individual sensor readings. Only detectable via composite logic.
composite_diagnostics.action: Immediate filter inspection — bypass allows unfiltered oil to reach air end bearings

composite_diagnostics.name: Thermal valve stuck open — early detection
composite_diagnostics.logic: IF (T1 < T1_expected_minus_8F) AND (load_pct > 50) AND (ambient_temp > 60F) THEN thermal_valve_stuck_open
composite_diagnostics.danger: Overcooling causes condensation in sump — fluid foaming — separator damage. T1 looks acceptably low so no alarm fires.
composite_diagnostics.action: Check thermal valve element, inspect fluid for water contamination

composite_diagnostics.name: Developing separator failure — pre-alarm signature
composite_diagnostics.danger: Separator dP alarm not yet triggered but trajectory indicates breach within projection window
composite_diagnostics.action: Plan separator replacement within 500 operating hours
